what_it_does: Generalizes the guided-review/walkthrough organ across every inbox lane, identifying which sequencer, presentation, and resolve pieces are shared and which framing branches or post-approval handlers differ per action class, while isolating build-review-specific logic.
observations: Lists nine inbox action types and their surfacing points from suite.py, grouped into three buckets by inbox_lanes().
Describes the walkthrough organ as four scheduler methods (start_session, present_current, next, start_walkthrough) plus a second entry point start_guide for ui:// element addresses.
Proposes lane-by-lane framing branches for deferred_offer, question, result, code/role/UI authoring classes, and governance-tier escalations.
Identifies shared components: one inbox queue, the go-gate graph/cursor sequencer, ui_target addressed markup, the resolve_surfaced closure path, and the walkthrough mode dial.
Lists instance-1-only build-review pieces: dispatch_decision on approve, surface_build_intent, decision_build consequence class, build-gate/scope-diff, and surface_intent_at.
Provides an ASCII architecture diagram showing one organ with N consumers created by branching in present_current.
Provides a named-consumer priority order for extending the organ.
Classifies evidence as Observed, Inferred, or Idea at the end of the file.
standouts: The central claim that all inbox lanes already ride the same organ and only need per-action framing branches in present_current.
start_guide is treated as a third consumer of the same organ rather than a separate system.
Explicit boundary drawn between general organ machinery and instance-1 build-review specifics.
Evidence notes tag claims by confidence level, distinguishing current code from unverified extension points.
conventions: Cites suite.py and bridge.py line numbers for observed behavior.
Separates observed code, inferred extension points, and speculative ideas into labeled categories.
Uses tables and an ASCII flow diagram to organize the architecture.
Follows a generalization-first structure: inventory, organ description, per-lane sketch, shared-vs-specific summary, priority list.
concerns: Several proposed present_current branches are explicitly marked as inferred or idea and not verified in the current code.
Assumes COA degrades gracefully for result and governance items without showing evidence.
The consumer priority order is a design recommendation, not derived from observed execution.
The file itself notes it contains no code symbols and that some paths do not yet exist.
notes: A design analysis arguing the guided-review sequencer is already general and only needs per-action framing branches, while keeping build-review-specific dispatch logic isolated.
questions: Is present_current currently structured to add these action-class branches without refactoring?
Does coa() actually degrade gracefully for plain content results and governance escalations?
Are deferred_offer payloads already carrying ui_target and revival state as assumed?
Does apply_surfaced already route all authoring action classes through a single resolve path?
What is the FE contract for answering a question via resolve_surfaced with a free-text reason?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file inventories inbox lanes, argues they all ride the same organ, and isolates build-review-only pieces.
apparent_themes: Generalize by reuse rather than duplication
One organ with per-consumer framing branches
Keep instance-1 specifics out of shared infrastructure
intention_signals: To show that the guided-review/walkthrough organ can support every inbox lane with minimal per-action branching, and to prevent build-review-specific dispatch and scope machinery from being baked into the general organ.
novelty: It treats ui:// guided tours and all nine inbox action classes as consumers of a single sequencer organ, and explicitly labels each claim as observed, inferred, or speculative idea.
contribution: The shared-organ abstraction, the per-action framing-branch taxonomy, the explicit instance-1-only boundary for build-review concerns, the evidence-confidence taxonomy, and the prioritized consumer rollout list.
summary_for_embedding: A second-consumer analysis mapping all nine inbox action lanes onto the same guided-review sequencer organ, identifying shared components and per-action framing branches while isolating build-review-specific dispatch logic.
intention_for_embedding: To justify generalizing the walkthrough organ across inbox lanes with minimal code changes and to keep instance-1 build-review concerns separate.
